name: New York Audiologist License
description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to New York - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: State Board for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
provider.parentOrganization: New York State Education Department (NYSED), Office of the Professions

address.description:
New York State Education Department
Office of the Professions
Speech-Language Pathology & Audiology Unit
89 Washington Avenue
Albany, NY 12234-1000

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a New York audiologist license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.
Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does New York have reciprocity for audiologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers audiologist practice in New York. To work in New York, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to New York?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in New York while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
